revision.comment: ページ [[塚原古墳]] を [[塚原古墳 (志摩市)]] へ移動: 塚原古墳と名付けられた古墳が他にも存在するため。
revision.text: #REDIRECT [[塚原古墳 (志摩市)]]

'''塚原古墳'''
# [[塚原古墳 (志摩市)]] [[三重県]]志摩市にあった円墳。現在消失。 # [[塚原古墳 (城南町)]] [[熊本県]]城南町にある古墳群。城南町立塚原古墳公園として、前方後円墳、方形周溝墓、円墳他77基の古墳群とともに整備されている。周辺地域も含めて調査済が203基、未調査のものを含めると500基近くあるのではと考えられている。

'''塚原古墳'''
# [[塚原古墳 (志摩市)]] [[三重県]]志摩市にあった円墳。現在消失。 # [[塚原古墳群 (城南町)]] [[熊本県]]城南町にある古墳群。城南町立塚原古墳公園として、前方後円墳、方形周溝墓、円墳他77基の古墳群とともに整備されている。周辺地域も含めて調査済が203基、未調査のものを含めると500基近くあるのではと考えられている。

'''塚原古墳'''
# [[塚原古墳群 (飯田市)]] [[長野県]][[飯田市]]にある古墳群。飯田市史跡に指定されている、全長約67mの前方後円墳である塚原二子塚古墳などがある。 # [[塚原古墳 (志摩市)]] [[三重県]][[志摩市]]にあった円墳。現在消失。 # [[塚原古墳群 (高槻市))]] かつては15群・約110基を数えた[[大阪府]][[高槻市]]の古墳群で、塚原八十塚と呼ばれていた。1872年、ウイリアム・ゴーランドによって有名となる。 # [[塚原古墳群 (城南町)]] [[熊本県]][[城南町]]にある古墳群。城南町立塚原古墳公園として、前方後円墳、方形周溝墓、円墳他77基の古墳群とともに整備されている。周辺地域も含めて調査済が203基、未調査のものを含めると500基近くあるのではと考えられている。

'''塚原古墳'''
* [[塚原古墳群 (多摩市)]] [[東京都]][[多摩市]]和田にある[[古墳群]]。[[古墳時代]]後期の古墳群。 * [[塚原古墳群 (飯田市)]] [[長野県]][[飯田市]]にある古墳群。飯田市史跡に指定されている、全長約67mの前方後円墳である塚原二子塚古墳などがある。 * [[塚原古墳 (志摩市)]] [[三重県]][[志摩市]]にあった円墳。現在消失。 * [[塚原古墳群 (高槻市)]] かつては15群・約110基を数えた[[大阪府]][[高槻市]]の古墳群で、塚原八十塚と呼ばれていた。[[1872年]]、[[ウィリアム・ゴーランド]]によって世界に発表され、古墳研究の先駆けになった古墳。 * [[塚原古墳群 (熊本市)]] [[熊本県]][[熊本市]]南区にある古墳群。国の史跡に指定。塚原古墳公園として、前方後円墳、方形周溝墓、円墳他77基の古墳群とともに整備されている。周辺地域も含めて調査済が203基、未調査のものを含めると500基近くあるのではと考えられている。 * [[岩原古墳群|塚原古墳 (山鹿市)]] 熊本県[[山鹿市]]にある9基の円墳からなる[[岩原古墳群]]のうちの一つ。国の史跡に指定。近くに県史跡指定の岩原横穴群がある。
{{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つかはらこふん}} [[Category:日本の古墳]]

revision.text:
'''Tsukahara Kofun'''
* [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Tama City)]]: A kofun cluster located in Wada, [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]]. It dates to the late [[Kofun period]]. * [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Iida City)]]: A cluster of kofun located in [[Iida City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It includes the Tsukahara Futatsuzuka Kofun, a keyhole-shaped tomb with a total length of approximately 67 meters, designated as a municipal historic site. * [[Tsukahara Kofun (Shima City)]]: A circular kofun that once existed in [[Shima City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], but has since been destroyed. * [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Takatsuki City)]]: A kofun cluster in [[Takatsuki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], formerly consisting of 15 groups and approximately 110 tombs known as the "Tsukahara Yasoizuka." It was first introduced to the world in 1872 by [[William Gowland]], marking a significant early milestone in kofun research. * [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Kumamoto City)]]: A kofun cluster in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], designated as a National Historic Site. The site includes 77 tombs, such as keyhole-shaped, square-ditched, and circular tombs, organized as part of Tsukahara Kofun Park. Including surrounding areas, 203 kofun have been investigated, with estimates suggesting nearly 500 kofun in total, including those yet to be surveyed. * [[Iwahara Kofun Cluster|Tsukahara Kofun (Yamaga City)]]: One of nine circular kofun that comprise the [[Iwahara Kofun Cluster]] in [[Yamaga City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. This kofun is designated as a National Historic Site, located near the Iwahara Horizontal Chamber Cluster, a prefectural historic site.
{{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukahara Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]]

revision.text:
'''Tsukahara Kofun'''
* [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Tama City)]]: A kofun cluster located in Wada, [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]]. It dates to the late [[Kofun period]]. * [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Iida City)]]: A cluster of kofun located in [[Iida City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It includes the Tsukahara Futatsuzuka Kofun, a keyhole-shaped tomb with a total length of approximately 67 meters, designated as a municipal historic site. * [[Tsukahara Kofun (Shima City)]]: A circular kofun that once existed in [[Shima City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], but has since been destroyed. * [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Takatsuki City)]]: A kofun cluster in [[Takatsuki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], formerly consisting of 15 groups and approximately 110 tombs known as the "Tsukahara Yasoizuka." It was first introduced to the world in 1872 by [[William Gowland]], marking a significant early milestone in kofun research. * [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Kumamoto City)]]: A kofun cluster in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], designated as a National Historic Site. The site includes 77 tombs, such as keyhole-shaped, square-ditched, and circular tombs, organized as part of Tsukahara Kofun Park. Including surrounding areas, 203 kofun have been investigated, with estimates suggesting nearly 500 kofun in total, including those yet to be surveyed. * [[Iwahara Kofun Cluster|Tsukahara Kofun (Yamaga City)]]: One of nine circular kofun that comprise the [[Iwahara Kofun Cluster]] in [[Yamaga City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. This kofun is designated as a National Historic Site, located near the Iwahara Horizontal Chamber Cluster, a prefectural historic site.
{{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukahara Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:塚原古墳]][[ja:塚原古墳]]

'''Tsukahara Kofun'''
* [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Tama City)]]: A kofun cluster located in Wada, [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]]. It dates to the late [[Kofun period]]. * [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Iida City)]]: A cluster of kofun located in [[Iida City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It includes the Tsukahara Futatsuzuka Kofun, a keyhole-shaped tomb with a total length of approximately 67 meters, designated as a municipal historic site. * [[Tsukahara Kofun (Shima City)]]: A circular kofun that once existed in [[Shima City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], but has since been destroyed. * [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Takatsuki City)]]: A kofun cluster in [[Takatsuki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], formerly consisting of 15 groups and approximately 110 tombs known as the "Tsukahara Yasoizuka." It was first introduced to the world in 1872 by [[William Gowland]], marking a significant early milestone in kofun research. * [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Kumamoto City)]]: A kofun cluster in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], designated as a National Historic Site. The site includes 77 tombs, such as keyhole-shaped, square-ditched, and circular tombs, organized as part of Tsukahara Kofun Park. Including surrounding areas, 203 kofun have been investigated, with estimates suggesting nearly 500 kofun in total, including those yet to be surveyed. * [[Iwahara Kofun Cluster|Tsukahara Kofun (Yamaga City)]]: One of nine circular kofun that comprise the [[Iwahara Kofun Cluster]] in [[Yamaga City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. This kofun is designated as a National Historic Site, located near the Iwahara Horizontal Chamber Cluster, a prefectural historic site.
{{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukahara Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:塚原古墳]][[category:new pages]][[ja:塚原古墳]]

'''Tsukahara Kofun'''
* [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Tama City)]]: A kofun cluster located in Wada, [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]]. It dates to the late [[Kofun period]]. * [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Iida City)]]: A cluster of kofun located in [[Iida City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It includes the Tsukahara Futatsuzuka Kofun, a keyhole-shaped tomb with a total length of approximately 67 meters, designated as a municipal historic site. * [[Tsukahara Kofun (Shima City)]]: A circular kofun that once existed in [[Shima City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], but has since been destroyed. * [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Takatsuki City)]]: A kofun cluster in [[Takatsuki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], formerly consisting of 15 groups and approximately 110 tombs known as the "Tsukahara Yasoizuka." It was first introduced to the world in 1872 by [[William Gowland]], marking a significant early milestone in kofun research. * [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Kumamoto City)]]: A kofun cluster in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], designated as a National Historic Site. The site includes 77 tombs, such as keyhole-shaped, square-ditched, and circular tombs, organized as part of Tsukahara Kofun Park. Including surrounding areas, 203 kofun have been investigated, with estimates suggesting nearly 500 kofun in total, including those yet to be surveyed. * [[Iwahara Kofun Cluster|Tsukahara Kofun (Yamaga City)]]: One of nine circular kofun that comprise the [[Iwahara Kofun Cluster]] in [[Yamaga City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. This kofun is designated as a National Historic Site, located near the Iwahara Horizontal Chamber Cluster, a prefectural historic site.
{{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukahara Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:塚原古墳]][[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:塚原古墳]]

'''Tsukahara Kofun'''
* [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Tama City)]]: A kofun cluster located in Wada, [[Tama City]], [[Tokyo]]. It dates to the late [[Kofun period]]. * [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Iida City)]]: A cluster of kofun located in [[Iida City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It includes the Tsukahara Futatsuzuka Kofun, a keyhole-shaped tomb with a total length of approximately 67 meters, designated as a municipal historic site. * [[Tsukahara Kofun (Shima City)]]: A circular kofun that once existed in [[Shima City]], [[Mie Prefecture]], but has since been destroyed. * [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Takatsuki City)]]: A kofun cluster in [[Takatsuki City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], formerly consisting of 15 groups and approximately 110 tombs known as the "Tsukahara Yasoizuka." It was first introduced to the world in 1872 by [[William Gowland]], marking a significant early milestone in kofun research. * [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster (Kumamoto City)]]: A kofun cluster in [[Kumamoto City]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], designated as a National Historic Site. The site includes 77 tombs, such as keyhole-shaped, square-ditched, and circular tombs, organized as part of Tsukahara Kofun Park. Including surrounding areas, 203 kofun have been investigated, with estimates suggesting nearly 500 kofun in total, including those yet to be surveyed. * [[Iwahara Kofun Cluster|Tsukahara Kofun (Yamaga City)]]: One of nine circular kofun that comprise the [[Iwahara Kofun Cluster]] in [[Yamaga City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. This kofun is designated as a National Historic Site, located near the Iwahara Horizontal Chamber Cluster, a prefectural historic site.
{{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukahara Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:塚原古墳]][[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:塚原古墳]]

'''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳)
* {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Tama)|ja|塚原古墳群 (多摩市)}} - Located in {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Prefecture|ja|東京都}} in the Wada area. A kofun group from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Iida)|ja|塚原古墳群 (飯田市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. Includes the Tsukahara Futagotsuka Kofun, a 67-meter-long keyhole-shaped kofun designated as a city historic site. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun (Shima)|ja|塚原古墳 (志摩市)}} - A circular kofun that once existed in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, now lost. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Takatsuki)|ja|塚原古墳群 (高槻市)}} - Located in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Once included 15 groups with about 110 kofun, collectively called Tsukahara Yajōzuka. Discovered by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}} in 1872, these kofun were pivotal in early research of ancient burial mounds. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Kumamoto)|ja|塚原古墳群 (熊本市)}} - Located in {{ill|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|ja|熊本市南区}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. A designated National Historic Site, developed as Tsukahara Kofun Park with 77 burial mounds including keyhole-shaped, square-ditch, and circular kofun. Surveys suggest around 500 kofun may exist in the area, with 203 confirmed. * {{ill|Iwahara Kofun Group|ja|岩原古墳群}} (Tsukahara Kofun in Yamaga) - Part of the Iwahara Kofun Group in {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|ja|山鹿市}}, Kumamoto. Consists of nine circular burial mounds designated as a National Historic Site. Nearby are the Iwahara Horizontal Burial Chambers, a prefectural historic site.
{{aimai}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[ja:塚原古墳]]

* {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Tama)|ja|塚原古墳群 (多摩市)}} - Located in {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Prefecture|ja|東京都}} in the Wada area. '''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳)
* {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Tama)|ja|塚原古墳群 (多摩市)}} - Located in {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Prefecture|ja|東京都}} in the Wada area. A kofun group from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Iida)|ja|塚原古墳群 (飯田市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. Includes the Tsukahara Futagotsuka Kofun, a 67-meter-long keyhole-shaped kofun designated as a city historic site. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun (Shima)|ja|塚原古墳 (志摩市)}} - A circular kofun that once existed in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, now lost. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Takatsuki)|ja|塚原古墳群 (高槻市)}} - Located in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Once included 15 groups with about 110 kofun, collectively called Tsukahara Yajōzuka. Discovered by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}} in 1872, these kofun were pivotal in early research of ancient burial mounds. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Kumamoto)|ja|塚原古墳群 (熊本市)}} - Located in {{ill|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|ja|熊本市南区}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. A designated National Historic Site, developed as Tsukahara Kofun Park with 77 burial mounds including keyhole-shaped, square-ditch, and circular kofun. Surveys suggest around 500 kofun may exist in the area, with 203 confirmed. * {{ill|Iwahara Kofun Group|ja|岩原古墳群}} (Tsukahara Kofun in Yamaga) - Part of the Iwahara Kofun Group in {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|ja|山鹿市}}, Kumamoto. Consists of nine circular burial mounds designated as a National Historic Site. Nearby are the Iwahara Horizontal Burial Chambers, a prefectural historic site.

'''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳)
* {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Tama)|ja|塚原古墳群 (多摩市)}} - Located in {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Prefecture|ja|東京都}} in the Wada area. A kofun group from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Iida)|ja|塚原古墳群 (飯田市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. Includes the Tsukahara Futagotsuka Kofun, a 67-meter-long keyhole-shaped kofun designated as a city historic site. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun (Shima)|ja|塚原古墳 (志摩市)}} - A circular kofun that once existed in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, now lost. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Takatsuki)|ja|塚原古墳群 (高槻市)}} - Located in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Once included 15 groups with about 110 kofun, collectively called Tsukahara Yajōzuka. Discovered by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}} in 1872, these kofun were pivotal in early research of ancient Kofuns. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Kumamoto)|ja|塚原古墳群 (熊本市)}} - Located in {{ill|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|ja|熊本市南区}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. A designated National Historic Site, developed as Tsukahara Kofun Park with 77 Kofuns including keyhole-shaped, square-ditch, and circular kofun. Surveys suggest around 500 kofun may exist in the area, with 203 confirmed. * {{ill|Iwahara Kofun Group|ja|岩原古墳群}} (Tsukahara Kofun in Yamaga) - Part of the Iwahara Kofun Group in {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|ja|山鹿市}}, Kumamoto. Consists of nine circular Kofuns designated as a National Historic Site. Nearby are the Iwahara Horizontal Burial Chambers, a prefectural historic site.

'''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳)
* {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Tama)|ja|塚原古墳群 (多摩市)}} - Located in {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|ja|多摩市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Prefecture|ja|東京都}} in the Wada area. A kofun group from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Iida)|ja|塚原古墳群 (飯田市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. Includes the Tsukahara Futagotsuka Kofun, a 67-meter-long keyhole-shaped kofun designated as a city historic site. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun (Shima)|ja|塚原古墳 (志摩市)}} - A circular kofun that once existed in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, now lost. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Takatsuki)|ja|塚原古墳群 (高槻市)}} - Located in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Once included 15 groups with about 110 kofun, collectively called Tsukahara Yajōzuka. Discovered by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド}} in 1872, these kofun were pivotal in early research of ancient Kofuns. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Kumamoto)|ja|塚原古墳群 (熊本市)}} - Located in {{ill|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|ja|熊本市南区}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. A designated National Historic Site, developed as Tsukahara Kofun Park with 77 Kofuns including keyhole-shaped, square-ditch, and circular kofun. Surveys suggest around 500 kofun may exist in the area, with 203 confirmed. * {{ill|Iwahara Kofun Group|ja|岩原古墳群}} (Tsukahara Kofun in Yamaga) - Part of the Iwahara Kofun Group in {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|ja|山鹿市}}, Kumamoto. Consists of nine circular Kofuns designated as a National Historic Site. Nearby are the Iwahara Horizontal Burial Chambers, a prefectural historic site.

A kofun group from the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Iida)|ja|塚原古墳群 (飯田市)|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}. Includes the Tsukahara Futagotsuka Kofun, a 67-meter-long keyhole-shaped kofun designated as a city historic site. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun (Shima)|ja|塚原古墳 (志摩市)|12=draft}} - A circular kofun that once existed in {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, now lost. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Takatsuki)|ja|塚原古墳群 (高槻市)|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Takatsuki|ja|高槻市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. Once included 15 groups with about 110 kofun, collectively called Tsukahara Yajōzuka. Discovered by {{ill|William Gowland|ja|ウィリアム・ゴーランド|12=draft}} in 1872, these kofun were pivotal in early research of ancient Kofuns. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Kumamoto)|ja|塚原古墳群 (熊本市)|12=draft}} - Located in {{ill|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|ja|熊本市南区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|12=draft}}. A designated National Historic Site, developed as Tsukahara Kofun Park with 77 Kofuns including keyhole-shaped, square-ditch, and circular kofun. Surveys suggest around 500 kofun may exist in the area, with 203 confirmed. * {{ill|Iwahara Kofun Group|ja|岩原古墳群|12=draft}} (Tsukahara Kofun in Yamaga) - Part of the Iwahara Kofun Group in {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|ja|山鹿市|12=draft}}, Kumamoto. Consists of nine circular Kofuns designated as a National Historic Site. Nearby are the Iwahara Horizontal Burial Chambers, a prefectural historic site.

revision.text:
'''塚原古墳'''
* [[塚原古墳群 (多摩市)]] [[東京都]][[多摩市]]和田にある[[古墳群]]。[[古墳時代]]後期の古墳群。 * [[塚原古墳群 (飯田市)]] [[長野県]][[飯田市]]にある古墳群。飯田市史跡に指定されている、全長約67mの前方後円墳である塚原二子塚古墳などがある。 * [[塚原古墳 (志摩市)]] [[三重県]][[志摩市]]にあった円墳。現在消失。 * [[塚原古墳群 (高槻市)]] かつては15群・約110基を数えた[[大阪府]][[高槻市]]の古墳群で、塚原八十塚と呼ばれていた。[[1872年]]、[[ウィリアム・ゴーランド]]によって世界に発表され、古墳研究の先駆けになった古墳。 * [[塚原古墳群 (熊本市)]] [[熊本県]][[熊本市]]南区にある古墳群。国の史跡に指定。塚原古墳公園として、前方後円墳、方形周溝墓、円墳他77基の古墳群とともに整備されている。周辺地域も含めて調査済が203基、未調査のものを含めると500基近くあるのではと考えられている。 * [[岩原古墳群|塚原古墳 (山鹿市)]] 熊本県[[山鹿市]]にある9基の円墳からなる[[岩原古墳群]]のうちの一つ。国の史跡に指定。近くに県史跡指定の岩原横穴群がある。
{{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つかはらこふん}} [[Category:日本の古墳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[塚原古墳]] to [[Tsukahara Kofun Cluster]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

'''塚原古墳'''
* [[塚原古墳群 (多摩市)]] [[東京都]][[多摩市]]和田にある[[古墳群]]。[[古墳時代]]後期の古墳群。 * [[塚原古墳群 (飯田市)]] [[長野県]][[飯田市]]にある古墳群。飯田市史跡に指定されている、全長約67mの前方後円墳である塚原二子塚古墳などがある。 * [[塚原古墳 (志摩市)]] [[三重県]][[志摩市]]にあった円墳。現在消失。 * [[塚原古墳群 (高槻市)]] かつては15群・約110基を数えた[[大阪府]][[高槻市]]の古墳群で、塚原八十塚と呼ばれていた。[[1872年]]、[[ウィリアム・ゴーランド]]によって世界に発表され、古墳研究の先駆けになった古墳。 * [[塚原古墳群 (熊本市)]] [[熊本県]][[熊本市]]南区にある古墳群。国の史跡に指定。塚原古墳公園として、前方後円墳、方形周溝墓、円墳他77基の古墳群とともに整備されている。周辺地域も含めて調査済が203基、未調査のものを含めると500基近くあるのではと考えられている。 * [[岩原古墳群|塚原古墳 (山鹿市)]] 熊本県[[山鹿市]]にある9基の円墳からなる[[岩原古墳群]]のうちの一つ。国の史跡に指定。近くに県史跡指定の岩原横穴群がある。

Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Tsukahara Kofun''' (塚原古墳)

* {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Tama)|ja|塚原古墳群 (多摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukahara Kofun Group (Tama)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Prefecture]] in the Wada area. A kofun group from the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
* {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Iida)|ja|塚原古墳群 (飯田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukahara Kofun Group (Iida)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. Includes the Tsukahara Futagotsuka Kofun, a 67-meter-long keyhole-shaped kofun designated as a city historic site.
* {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun (Shima)|ja|塚原古墳 (志摩市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukahara Kofun (Shima)|qq=}} - A circular kofun that once existed in [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], now lost.
* {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Takatsuki)|ja|塚原古墳群 (高槻市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukahara Kofun Group (Takatsuki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Located in [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Once included 15 groups with about 110 kofun, collectively called Tsukahara Yajōzuka. Discovered by [[:en:William Gowland|William Gowland]] in 1872, these kofun were pivotal in early research of ancient Kofuns.
* [[:en:Tsukawara Kofun Cluster|Tsukahara Kofun Group (Kumamoto)]] - Located in {{ill|Minami-ku, Kumamoto|ja|熊本市南区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-ku, Kumamoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. A designated National Historic Site, developed as Tsukahara Kofun Park with 77 Kofuns including keyhole-shaped, square-ditch, and circular kofun. Surveys suggest around 500 kofun may exist in the area, with 203 confirmed.
* {{ill|Iwahara Kofun Group|ja|岩原古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwahara Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578830}} (Tsukahara Kofun in Yamaga) - Part of the Iwahara Kofun Group in [[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga, Kumamoto]], Kumamoto. Consists of nine circular Kofuns designated as a National Historic Site. Nearby are the Iwahara Horizontal Burial Chambers, a prefectural historic site.

A designated National Historic Site, developed as Tsukahara Kofun Park with 77 Kofuns including keyhole-shaped, square-ditch, and circular kofun. Surveys suggest around 500 kofun may exist in the area, with 203 confirmed.
* {{ill|Iwahara Kofun Group|ja|岩原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwahara Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578830}} (Tsukahara Kofun in Yamaga) - Part of the Iwahara Kofun Group in [[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga, Kumamoto]], Kumamoto. Consists of nine circular Kofuns designated as a National Historic Site. Nearby are the Iwahara Horizontal Burial Chambers, a prefectural historic site.
